_meta.label: Eris Protocol
_meta.summary: Terra liquid-staking & vault platform behind the Alliance Liquidity Hub (TLA). 'RealYield, no platform token' — value accrues into amp[TOKEN] itself.

facts.claim: Eris accrues value into the amp[TOKEN] itself rather than a separate governance/platform token.

ampLUNA is an auto-compounding liquid-staking receipt: deposit LUNA, Eris stakes it with whitelisted validators, harvests and restakes rewards daily, so the underlying LUNA per ampLUNA rises continuously.

Because ampLUNA's value is plain staking yield, its redemption price (LUNA × hub ratio) tracks its market price tightly — it is the textbook 'clean staking derivative'. Larger transient gaps are almost always snapshot timing, not real divergence.
facts.notes: Confirmed in our own runs: an 8% reading collapsed to 0.5% one snapshot later — pure timing. This is why our LST review threshold is 10%, not 2%.

facts.claim: ampLUNA (Amplifier) fees: deposit 0%, performance 5% to Protocol Treasury, withdrawal 0%.

arbLUNA is an arbitrage vault, NOT a staking receipt. It captures the ~1-2% premium between instantly swapping an LST and waiting out the 21-24 day unbond, executing only profitable arbitrage each ~21-25 day cycle and compounding the profit into the token. Its value grows only through captured market inefficiencies — no minted token, no incentives.
facts.notes: This is the key fact for understanding why arbLUNA's price behaves unlike ampLUNA's.

facts.claim: arbLUNA is a 'strategy LST': its hub ratio reflects accumulated arbitrage profit (not staking yield), so its ratio is higher and its market price can legitimately diverge from a naive read.
Redeeming arbLUNA back to LUNA takes 25 days (normal unbond).
Instant withdrawal of arbLUNA (when contract liquidity is available) costs a 5% markup fee, dropping linearly to zero as the position reaches its 25-day unlock.
The Arb Vault deliberately targets keeping roughly three-quarters of capital unbonding for efficiency, so instantly-withdrawable idle liquidity is limited by design.

arbLUNA's liquid market price can sit below its redemption value because realizing redemption requires either a 25-day wait or up to a 5% instant-exit fee. The market prices in this time-lock / exit-cost friction. This is expected behavior, not a depeg or a data error.
facts.notes: Explains the DIRECTION and CAUSE of the gap. The SIZE is a separate live_binding (arbluna.market_vs_redemption) — never hardcode the percentage.

facts.claim: arbLUNA (Arb Vault) fees: deposit 0%, performance 5% to holders + 5% to Treasury (10% total, higher because arbitrage needs complex bots), withdrawal 0.5% (shared with ampLUNA holders).

arbLUNA's APY cannot be calculated in advance — it depends on how often arbitrage opportunities appear and their size. Eris publishes an illustrative table (e.g. a 1% opportunity every 21 days ≈ 18.9% APY; 2% ≈ 41%).

ampLP is an amplified LP position (Amp Compounder): deposit an Astroport LP, a controller bot claims and restakes pool rewards daily while NO new ampLP are minted, so the ampLP-to-LP conversion rate rises every day.

ampLP must be valued by its share of the underlying LP (LP-conversion-rate × the LP's reserves-based intrinsic value), NOT as amount × per-unit price. Its design is fixed supply with rising backing, so the per-unit-price model is provably wrong.
facts.notes: Authoritative source for our LP/ampLP share-fraction valuation doctrine.

facts.claim: ampLP (Amp Compounder) fees: deposit 0%, performance 2.5% to amp[TOKEN] holders + 2.5% Treasury (5% total), withdrawal 0%.
Amp Governance issues vAMP (vote-escrowed ampLUNA) for governance — this is the VOTING_ESCROW / vAMP minter referenced in our contracts config.
The Alliance Liquidity Hub is the TLA primitive itself — Eris-operated infrastructure that turns staking rewards into liquidity incentives via community voting.

Eris contracts have independent third-party security audits. SCV Security publishes audits of Amplified Staking, amp-compounder, ve3 contracts, Tokenfactory, Phoenix Treasury, and Ampz. Oak Security audited the Amplified Staking Hub. These are public and verifiable.
facts.notes: Also: https://github.com/oak-security/audit-reports/tree/master/Eris%20Protocol . Source code at github.com/erisprotocol (contracts-terra = staking hub; contracts-ve3 = TLA ve3 contracts).

facts.claim: Eris contract source code is open: github.com/erisprotocol/contracts-terra (Amplified Staking hub — the ampLUNA/bLUNA exchange-rate mechanism) and github.com/erisprotocol/contracts-ve3 (the TLA ve3 asset-staking + voting-escrow + global-config contracts).
ampLUNA's current on-chain hub exchange rate (accumulated LUNA per ampLUNA).
facts.notes: Rises daily; never hardcode. Query is {exchange_rates:{}} on the ampLUNA hub.

facts.claim: The current gap between arbLUNA's market price and its redemption value, as measured by our token-catalog cron.
facts.notes: Changes constantly. Show current value and, as snapshot history accrues, the observed range. A value within normal range = healthy friction pricing; far outside = review (thin pool or real event).
